What has keys but can't open locks? A piano.
What has hands but can't clap? A clock.
What runs but never walks, has a mouth but never talks? A river.
What has one eye but cannot see? A needle.
What gets wet while drying? A towel.
What goes up but never comes down? Your age.
What has a head and a tail but no body? A coin.
What belongs to you but is used by others more than you? Your name.
What has many teeth but cannot bite? A comb.
What can travel around the world while staying in a corner? A stamp.
What gets bigger the more you take away? A hole.
What has four wheels and flies? A garbage truck.
What can you catch but not throw? A cold.
What has an eye but can’t see? A hurricane.
What is full of holes but still holds water? A sponge.
What goes through cities and fields but never moves? A road.
What can fill a room but takes up no space? Light.
What has a neck but no head? A bottle.
What has an arm but no hand? A chair.
What has a bottom at the top? Your legs.
What gets sharper the more you use it? Your brain.
What kind of band never plays music? A rubber band.
What goes up and down but doesn’t move? A staircase.
I’m tall when I’m young, and I’m short when I’m old. What am I? A candle.
What invention lets you look right through a wall? A window.
What has cities but no houses, forests without trees, and rivers without water? A map.
What is always in front of you but can’t be seen? The future.
What can you break, even if you never pick it up or touch it? A promise.
What begins with T, ends with T, and has T in it? A teapot.
What building has the most stories? A library.
What goes up when rain comes down? An umbrella.
What has a heart that doesn’t beat? An artichoke.
What has a thumb and four fingers but isn’t alive? A glove.
What moves faster: heat or cold? Heat, because you can catch a cold.
What has roots nobody sees, is taller than trees, up, up it goes, and yet never grows? A mountain.
What can you hold in your left hand but not in your right? Your right elbow.
What goes in hard and dry and comes out soft and wet? Pasta.
What word is spelled wrong in every dictionary? Wrong.
What has feet but no legs? A snail.
What has an end but no beginning? A stick.
Where does today come before yesterday? The dictionary.
What gets broken without being held? A promise.
What weighs more, a pound of feathers or a pound of bricks? Neither—they both weigh a pound.
What has a face and two hands but no arms or legs? A clock.
What can point in every direction but can’t reach the destination by itself? A compass.
What runs around the yard without moving? A fence.
What has to be broken before you can use it? An egg.
